original_text:
### Modular Assembly Conventions **[GENERAL — Solstice production rules]**
- Each section is a self-contained `<table>`-based block at 600px with its own top/bottom 32px padding and declared background color; **no margins outside the section** — assembly is direct stacking.
- Section metadata must declare: background color, whether the section establishes `dermatomyositis (DM)`, footnote symbols consumed (†, ‡ assigned at assembly to avoid collisions), and whether it contains a CTA.
- Footnote symbols: superscript, assigned in order of appearance at **assembly time** — sections use placeholder tokens (e.g., `{{fn1}}`) rather than hard-coded daggers when more than one referenced claim exists.
- Locked components (header, footer, ISI) are inserted by the core team; sections adjacent to them must not include decorative elements that visually depend on what's above/below (e.g., a wave that's meant to continue into the footer).
- Dark-background sections must not be placed adjacent to other dark sections (assembler responsibility, enabled by declared backgrounds).
- All colors in code as HEX; all sizing in px; inline CSS only (email client compatibility); `bgcolor` attributes duplicated for Outlook.

relations.note: Declared background color in metadata is what enables the assembler to enforce dark-section adjacency.
Declared consumed footnote symbols feed the assembly-time ordered assignment.

quotes: Each section is a self-contained `<table>`-based block at 600px with its own top/bottom 32px padding and declared background color
no margins outside the section** — assembly is direct stacking

rules.intent: Guarantee modular sections stack cleanly across email clients.
rules.rule_text: Each section is a self-contained `<table>`-based block at 600px (size.canvas.email_body_width_600) with its own top/bottom 32px padding (section.padding.top_bottom) and a declared background color. There are no margins outside the section — assembly is direct stacking.

rules.intent: Provide the metadata the assembler needs to compose sections safely.

quotes: assigned in order of appearance at **assembly time**

Footnote symbols: superscript, assigned in order of appearance at **assembly time** — sections use placeholder tokens (e.g., `{{fn1}}`) rather than hard-coded daggers when more than one referenced claim exists.

rules.intent: Avoid footnote-symbol collisions when modular sections are combined.
rules.rule_text: Footnote symbols are superscript and assigned in order of appearance at assembly time. Sections use placeholder tokens (e.g., {{fn1}}) rather than hard-coded daggers when more than one referenced claim exists.

quotes: sections adjacent to them must not include decorative elements that visually depend on what's above/below (e.g., a wave that's meant to continue into the footer)

rules.intent: Prevent broken visuals where authored sections meet core-team locked components.
rules.rule_text: Locked components (header, footer, ISI) are inserted by the core team; sections adjacent to them must not include decorative elements that visually depend on what's above or below (e.g., a wave meant to continue into the footer).

quotes: Dark-background sections must not be placed adjacent to other dark sections
Dark-background sections must not be placed adjacent to other dark sections (assembler responsibility, enabled by declared backgrounds).

rules.intent: Maintain visual rhythm and contrast between stacked sections.
rules.rule_text: Dark-background sections must not be placed adjacent to other dark sections (assembler responsibility, enabled by declared backgrounds).

quotes: All colors in code as HEX; all sizing in px; inline CSS only (email client compatibility); `bgcolor` attributes duplicated for Outlook.

rules.intent: Ensure email-client compatibility across renderers including Outlook.
rules.rule_text: All colors in code as HEX; all sizing in px; inline CSS only (for email client compatibility); bgcolor attributes duplicated for Outlook.
